plant.common_name: Pastries (radish cake, rice cake, pineapple cake, mochi)
plant.scientific_name: Pastries (radish cake, rice cake, pineapple cake, mochi)
plant.description:
Brief Description and Common Forms
Description: Pastries are highly processed human snacks and traditional festive foods. The sources strongly warn against feeding these items to cats and dogs. Because they are made with high amounts of starch and glutinous rice, they are difficult for pets to digest and easily cause flatulence (bloating) and gastrointestinal discomfort1. Furthermore, their highly sticky texture presents a severe choking hazard, which can trigger life-threatening emergencies if a pet attempts to swallow them1.
Common Forms: In households, pets are most likely to encounter these as human snacks or traditional holiday treats. The specific forms listed in the sources include radish cake (蘿蔔糕), rice cake (年糕), steamed sponge cake / fa gao (發糕), pineapple cake (鳳梨酥), and mochi (麻糬)1.

toxins.name: Based on the provided sources, pastries such as radish cake, rice cake, pineapple cake
High Starch and Glutinous Rice (Complex Carbohydrates)
toxins.description:
in cats: Traditional festive pastries are primarily formulated from glutinous rice and high amounts of starch1. Because cats are obligate carnivores, their digestive systems are not equipped to break down and metabolize heavy loads of complex carbohydrates1. Ingesting these ingredients easily leads to severe flatulence (bloating) and acute gastrointestinal discomfort1.
Any notes on concentration or potency: The sources do not specify a toxic dose but warn that because these foods are "mostly made of glutinous rice and high starch," the entire food item is fundamentally unsuitable for a cat's digestive system
toxins.concentration_notes: or potency: The sources do not specify a toxic dose but warn that because these foods are "mostly made of glutinous rice and high starch," the entire food item is fundamentally unsuitable for a cat's digestive system

toxins.name: Highly Sticky Dough (Physical Hazard)
toxins.description:
in cats: Because of how items like mochi and rice cakes are prepared, they possess a highly sticky and dense texture1. Rather than chemically poisoning the cat, this presents a severe physical choking hazard1. If a cat attempts to swallow these sticky foods, they can easily become lodged in the throat or esophagus, triggering a life-threatening emergency1.
Any notes on concentration or potency: N/A. The danger is not dose-dependent but relies entirely on the physical consistency and stickiness of the pastry
toxins.concentration_notes: or potency: N/A. The danger is not dose-dependent but relies entirely on the physical consistency and stickiness of the pastry

symptoms.name: Based on the sources, pastries such as radish cake, rice cake, pineapple cake, and mochi do not contain a specific chemical poison
Flatulence and Gastrointestinal Discomfort
symptoms.onset: Within a few hours of ingestion (as digestion begins).
symptoms.notes: These pastries are primarily made from high amounts of starch and glutinous rice12. A cat's digestive system is not well-equipped to process heavy loads of complex carbohydrates, which makes these items difficult to digest and leads to acute stomach upset and gas

symptoms.name: Choking / Airway Obstruction
symptoms.onset: Immediate (during the act of chewing and swallowing).
symptoms.notes: Because of their preparation, these foods—especially mochi and rice cakes—possess a highly sticky and dense physical texture12. This stickiness presents a severe physical hazard, as the pastry can easily become lodged in the cat's throat, triggering a life-threatening emergency

treatments.name: Because the provided sources do not identify a specific chemical poison in pastries
Emergency Airway Clearance / Obstruction Removal (If choking occurs)
treatments.description:
of the procedure: If the sticky food mass becomes lodged in the cat's throat or creates a blockage in the digestive tract, the veterinarian must intervene immediately to clear the airway, or potentially perform surgery to safely remove the obstructing material12.
Any situation-specific
treatments.notes: The sources explicitly warn that the dense, highly sticky texture of these festive pastries (especially mochi and rice cakes) presents a severe choking hazard for pets, making this a hyper-acute, life-threatening emergency

treatments.name: Symptomatic Gastrointestinal Support (For severe bloating and GI upset)
treatments.description:
of the procedure: If the cat successfully swallows the pastry but develops severe gastrointestinal distress, the veterinarian will provide symptomatic and supportive care (such as fluid therapy or anti-nausea medications) to keep the cat stable and hydrated until the inflammation and symptoms resolve13.
Any situation-specific
treatments.notes: These pastries are primarily made from high amounts of starch and glutinous rice1. Because cats are obligate carnivores, their digestive systems are not equipped to process heavy loads of carbohydrates4. This inability to properly metabolize the ingredients routinely leads to severe flatulence (bloating) and acute intestinal discomfort
